title: mentha rocheliana
intro: Mentha rocheliana, also known as Corsican mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its small, rounded leaves and pink or white flowers. The medicinal properties of Mentha rocheliana include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating digestive issues, fever, and wounds. The herbal preparations of Mentha rocheliana include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as provide relief from stress and anxiety.
